index.title: Odoo–Shopify Order Check
index.description: Odoo–Shopify Order Check - Compare any order between Odoo and Shopify field by field, get an instant verdict and a clear discrepancy breakdown. Read-only Chrome extension.
index.ogDescription: Catch order mismatches between Odoo and Shopify before they cost you. Field-by-field comparison, a verdict based on what's invoiced, and a discrepancy breakdown — read-only.
index.twitterDescription: Field-by-field order comparison between Odoo and Shopify, with an instant verdict and discrepancy breakdown. Read-only.

pricing.title: Pricing - Odoo–Shopify Order Check
pricing.description: Odoo–Shopify Order Check pricing - Start with a 14-day free trial, then pick Starter, Growth or Agency. Plans are scoped by Odoo instance and Shopify stores, company-wide with no per-user limit. Read-only field-by-field order comparison.
pricing.ogDescription: 14-day free trial, then Starter, Growth or Agency — scoped by Odoo instance and Shopify stores, company-wide. Field-by-field order comparison between Odoo and Shopify — read-only.
pricing.twitterDescription: 14-day free trial, then Starter, Growth or Agency — company-wide plans scoped by Odoo instance and Shopify stores. Read-only order comparison between Odoo and Shopify.

userGuide.title: User Guide - Odoo–Shopify Order Check
userGuide.description: Complete user guide for Odoo–Shopify Order Check. Learn how to install it, read the match/mismatch verdict and discrepancy breakdown, why the verdict uses the net invoiced amount, how refunds and cancellations show as context, and how to manage your subscription.
userGuide.ogDescription: Install Odoo–Shopify Order Check and read its verdict with confidence: discrepancy breakdown, invoiced vs current, refunds and cancellations as context, and subscription management.
userGuide.twitterDescription: Complete guide to install and use Odoo–Shopify Order Check: verdict, discrepancy breakdown, invoiced vs current, and subscription.

changelog.description: Release history for the Odoo–Shopify Order Check Chrome extension. Track new features, improvements, and fixes.
changelog.ogDescription: Release history and updates for Odoo–Shopify Order Check.
changelog.twitterDescription: Release history and updates for Odoo–Shopify Order Check.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy - Odoo–Shopify Order Check
privacy.description: Privacy Policy for Odoo–Shopify Order Check. Read-only Chrome extension — learn what order data we read, what stays on your device, what permissions we request, and your privacy rights.
privacy.ogDescription: How the read-only Odoo–Shopify Order Check extension handles your data. No order data is sent to the developer or any third party.
privacy.twitterDescription: How we handle your data and your privacy rights. Read-only, no order data leaves your Odoo and Shopify.

header.title: User Guide
header.subtitle: Everything you need to install Odoo–Shopify Order Check and read its verdict with confidence.

installation.title: Installation
installation.chromeVersion: Google Chrome (or any Chromium-based browser) with extensions enabled.
installation.odooVersion: Odoo 14–19+, on Odoo Online (*.odoo.com) or Odoo.sh, with a user account you can sign in to.
installation.shopifyVersion: A Shopify store connected to Odoo through a supported connector (currently Emipro).
installation.step1Title: Open the Chrome Web Store listing
installation.step1Desc: Go to the Odoo–Shopify Order Check page on the Chrome Web Store.
installation.step2Desc: Click "Add to Chrome" and confirm the prompt. The extension installs in a couple of seconds.
installation.step3Desc: Pin the extension to your toolbar so the settings popup is always one click away.
installation.step4Desc: Open your Odoo instance in the same browser and sign in. That's the only step needed before you start comparing orders.
installation.noteDesc: The extension never writes to Odoo or Shopify. It only reads order data to build the comparison, so it is safe to run on production.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.intro: There is nothing to configure. Once the extension is installed and you are signed in to Odoo, open an order and the comparison appears automatically.
gettingStarted.step1Desc: Open your Odoo instance and log in as usual. The extension reads your active session to detect the instance and database — no API key, no URL to type.
gettingStarted.step2Desc: Open any sales order in Odoo (or the matching order in Shopify). The extension auto-detects it and injects a comparison panel.
gettingStarted.step3Desc: The panel shows a global match or mismatch verdict, then lets you expand the field-by-field breakdown on demand.
gettingStarted.zeroConfigDesc: There are no instances to register, no API keys to paste and no options page. The instance and database come straight from your Odoo browser session, cookie-first.
gettingStarted.readOnlyDesc: All network requests run in the extension's service worker. The Shopify Admin token is read from Odoo through the connector, kept in memory only, and never persisted or exposed to the page.

verdict.title: Reading the Verdict
verdict.intro: The top of the panel gives you a single, at-a-glance verdict for the order:
verdict.matchDesc: Odoo and Shopify agree on the order. No discrepancies were found across the compared fields.
verdict.mismatchDesc: At least one field differs between the two systems. The breakdown lists exactly what and where.
verdict.errorDesc: The extension couldn't build a comparison — for example, your Odoo session expired or the order isn't linked to Shopify yet. The panel tells you what to do next.
verdict.expandDesc: The verdict is immediate; the breakdown is on demand. Expand the panel to see every compared field, the value on each side, and a context section for anything that explains a difference without being an error.

breakdown.title: The Discrepancy Breakdown
breakdown.intro: When there's a mismatch, the breakdown shows you field by field where Odoo and Shopify disagree:
breakdown.totalsDesc: The order total, compared against Odoo's net invoiced amount (see the next section for why).
breakdown.taxesDesc: Tax amounts on each side. On orders compared against the invoiced total, the tax base and taxes may be marked as not comparable in this version.
breakdown.quantitiesDesc: Quantities per line. Odoo's invoiced quantity is treated as the authority against Shopify's current quantity.
breakdown.linesDesc: Products matched by SKU, with connector artifacts (such as discount or gift lines) excluded so they don't show up as phantom differences.
breakdown.addressesDesc: Shipping and billing addresses, compared by their meaningful parts (street, city, ZIP) rather than a single display name.
breakdown.severityDesc: Each discrepancy is weighted, so a rounding-level difference doesn't read the same as a missing line or a wrong total. The verdict reflects the most significant difference found.

invoiced.title: Invoiced vs Current
invoiced.intro: The economic verdict compares Shopify's current order against what has actually been invoiced in Odoo — not against the original sale order.
invoiced.whyDesc: When an order is invoiced, the connector freezes the sale order. Comparing that frozen order against a Shopify order that has since changed produces false positives. Instead, the extension uses Odoo's net invoiced amount — posted customer invoices minus posted refunds — which is what genuinely matches Shopify's current state.
invoiced.odooSideDesc: Net invoiced total (sum of posted customer invoices minus posted refunds) and the invoiced quantity per line as the authority.
invoiced.shopifySideDesc: The current order total and current quantity per line — the live source of truth for what the customer actually has.
invoiced.preInvoiceDesc: If the order has no posted invoices, the extension falls back to comparing the ordered amounts and labels the verdict as provisional so you know an invoice will firm it up later.

context.title: Refunds, Returns & Cancellations
context.intro: Refunds, returns and order edits rarely mean something is broken — they're part of normal order life. The extension shows them as context, kept separate from the discrepancy list so they don't trigger false mismatches:
context.refundsDesc: The original amount, the refunds applied, and the resulting net invoiced amount are shown together so the math is transparent.
context.returnsDesc: A return in progress is shown as context on the fulfilment side rather than counted as a quantity discrepancy.
context.editsDesc: When a Shopify order is edited, the history (original, refunds, edits) is shown so a legitimate change doesn't look like an error.
context.cancelledDesc: When an order is cancelled, the panel shows a clear banner so you immediately understand why the numbers look the way they do, instead of reading the cancellation as a mismatch.
context.noteDesc: Everything in this section is informational. It explains differences; it never counts toward the match or mismatch verdict on its own.

fromShopify.title: Starting from Shopify
fromShopify.intro: You can open the comparison from a Shopify order too — not just from Odoo. To do that, the extension needs to know which Odoo instance the store belongs to, and it learns that mapping the first time you compare the order from Odoo.
fromShopify.step1: Sign in to Odoo and open the order there once. The extension resolves the store and remembers which Odoo instance and database it maps to.
fromShopify.step2: From then on, opening the matching order in Shopify works directly — the extension already knows where to look.
fromShopify.step3: The mapping is stored locally in your browser; nothing is sent anywhere.
fromShopify.notLearnedDesc: If you open a Shopify order for a store the extension hasn't seen from Odoo, it asks you to open the order in Odoo first instead of guessing. Once it has learned the mapping, Shopify-first comparisons just work.

settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: A small settings home lets you check your access status, manage your license and read a few diagnostics — without leaving the page you're on.
settings.popupDesc: Click the extension icon in your toolbar to open settings from any tab.
settings.gearDesc: Open the ⚙ gear in the comparison panel to see the same settings in place, right next to the order you're looking at.
settings.what1Desc: Check whether you're on the free trial or an active license, and how many trial days remain.
settings.what2Desc: Paste a Polar license key to unlock the extension at any time — during the trial or after it ends.
settings.what3Desc: Jump straight to the checkout to purchase a Starter, Growth or Agency plan.
settings.what4Desc: Open Polar's hosted customer portal to change or cancel your subscription.
settings.diagnosticsDesc: The diagnostics panel is purely informational and shows:

subscription.title: Subscription
subscription.trialDesc: Every install starts with a 14-day free trial that unlocks the full extension. The trial is tracked locally on your device on an honor system — no sign-up and no network call to start it.
subscription.starterDesc: Covers 1 Odoo instance and 1 Shopify store.
subscription.growthDesc: Covers 1 Odoo instance and unlimited Shopify stores.
subscription.agencyDesc: Covers unlimited Odoo instances and Shopify stores.
subscription.buyStep1: Open settings from the extension icon popup or the ⚙ gear in the panel.
subscription.buyStep2: Choose Buy and pick the Starter, Growth or Agency plan.
subscription.buyStep3: Complete the checkout. You'll receive a license key to activate the extension.
subscription.activateStep1: Open settings and find the license key field.
subscription.activateStep2: Paste the key you received after checkout.
subscription.activateStep3: Activate. The full extension unlocks immediately for your whole team.
subscription.companyWideDesc: One subscription covers your whole company — the same license key works across your team, with no per-user limit. Plans differ only in scope: how many Odoo instances and Shopify stores they cover.
subscription.manageDesc: Billing, plan changes and cancellation are handled in Polar's hosted customer portal — the extension simply links you there.
subscription.manageStep1: Open settings from the popup or the panel gear.
subscription.manageStep2: Choose Manage subscription to open the Polar customer portal.
subscription.manageStep3: Update your plan, payment details or cancel from there.
subscription.manageNote: The extension never stores your payment details. All billing lives in Polar's portal.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.noSessionDesc: If you're not signed in to Odoo, the extension can't detect your instance and shows an actionable "sign in to Odoo" message instead of guessing.
troubleshooting.noSessionStep1: Open your Odoo instance in the same browser.
troubleshooting.noSessionStep3: Return to the order — the panel picks up your session automatically.
troubleshooting.notLinked1Desc: If the order has no Shopify counterpart, there's nothing to compare and the panel quietly steps aside.
troubleshooting.notLinked2Desc: When starting from a Shopify store the extension hasn't seen yet, open the order in Odoo once so it can learn the store-to-instance mapping.
troubleshooting.notDetected1Desc: If your Odoo session has expired, sign back in and reopen the order.
troubleshooting.notDetected2Desc: Autodetection targets Odoo Online (*.odoo.com) and Odoo.sh in this version. Odoo on a fully custom domain isn't covered yet.
troubleshooting.panelReloadDesc: Refresh the order tab so the content script re-runs and re-detects the order.
troubleshooting.panelEnabledDesc: Open chrome://extensions and confirm Odoo–Shopify Order Check is turned on.
troubleshooting.noteDesc: The diagnostics in settings show the detected Odoo instance and your extension version — handy details to include when you contact support.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What does Odoo–Shopify Order Check do?
faq.a1: When you open an order in Odoo or Shopify, the extension compares both systems field by field — totals, taxes, quantities, line items and addresses — and shows a global match or mismatch verdict with a breakdown of any discrepancies.
faq.q2: Does it change anything in Odoo or Shopify?
faq.a2: No. The extension is strictly read-only. It never creates, edits or deletes data in either system — it only reads, so it is safe to run on production.
faq.q3: Do I need to configure anything?
faq.a3: No. The extension auto-detects your Odoo instance and database from your active session. There are no instances to register, no API keys and no options page.
faq.a4: Yes. Every install includes a 14-day free trial with full functionality. After the trial, the Starter, Growth and Agency plans are available — each covers your whole team and is scoped by how many Odoo instances and Shopify stores it includes.
faq.a5: Open settings, choose Manage subscription, and cancel from Polar's hosted customer portal. The extension only links you there; it never stores payment details.
faq.q6: Where does the Shopify token come from?
faq.a6: It's read from Odoo through the connector and kept in memory only inside the extension's service worker. It is never persisted to disk or exposed to the page you're viewing.
faq.q7: Does my order data leave my browser?
faq.a7: There's no backend. The extension reads Odoo and Shopify directly from your browser to build the comparison, and the learned store-to-instance mapping is stored locally on your device.

help.title: Getting Help
help.contactDesc: If something isn't working or you have a question, reach out and we'll help:
help.includeVersion: Include your extension version (shown in the settings diagnostics).
help.includeScreenshots: Screenshots of the panel and the order make it much faster to help.

autoLogging.title: Refunds & returns in context

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: Odoo–Shopify Order Check ("the Extension") is committed to protecting your privacy. This privacy policy explains what information the Extension reads, how it is used, and how it is protected.
overview.readOnly: The Extension is strictly read-only. It never creates, edits, or deletes anything in Odoo or Shopify — it only reads, so it is safe to run on production.

infoCollect.title: Information We Read
infoCollect.intro: The Extension reads order data only when you open an order. All network requests are made from the Extension's service worker — never from the page you are viewing.
infoCollect.odooDataDesc: Using your active Odoo browser session (cookie-first), the Extension reads the following from YOUR Odoo instance to build the comparison:
infoCollect.odooData1: Sale order details: totals, taxes, quantities, and line items
infoCollect.odooData2: Invoiced and refunded amounts used to compute the net invoiced verdict
infoCollect.odooData3: Billing and shipping address fields for the order
infoCollect.odooData4: Connector configuration that links the order to your Shopify store (read from your Odoo instance)
infoCollect.shopifyDataDesc: Through Shopify's Admin API, the Extension reads the matching order from YOUR Shopify store to compare it against Odoo:
infoCollect.shopifyData1: Current order totals, taxes, and quantities
infoCollect.shopifyData2: Line items, refunds, returns, and order edits used as context
infoCollect.shopifyData3: Order and customer address fields for the order
infoCollect.tokenNote: The Shopify Admin token is obtained from your Odoo connector configuration and kept in memory in the service worker only. It is never persisted to disk and is never exposed to the page or DOM.
infoCollect.localDataDesc: The Extension stores a small amount of operational data locally using Chrome's storage (chrome.storage.local):
infoCollect.localData1: Detected Odoo session info (base URL and database)
infoCollect.localData2: A learned shop-to-instance mapping (which Shopify store maps to which Odoo instance)
infoCollect.localData3: The trial install date (used to track the free trial period)
infoCollect.localData4: License activation state for your subscription
infoCollect.noOrderData: Order details read from Odoo and Shopify are used only to produce the comparison in your browser. They are not stored beyond transient caching to keep the panel responsive.

howWeUse.title: How We Use Your Information
howWeUse.intro: The Extension uses the information it reads solely for the following purposes:
howWeUse.compareDesc: Match the same order between Odoo and Shopify field by field — totals, taxes, quantities, line items, and addresses
howWeUse.verdictDesc: Show a global match/mismatch verdict based on the net invoiced amount (invoices minus refunds), with refunds, returns, and edits shown as context
howWeUse.cachingDesc: Temporarily cache responses in the service worker to keep the comparison panel responsive while you stay on the same order
howWeUse.licensingDesc: Validate your license key and track your free trial so you can use the Extension after the trial period

storage.title: Data Storage and Security
storage.readOnlyDesc: The Extension only reads from Odoo and Shopify. It never creates, edits, or deletes any record in either system.
storage.serviceWorkerDesc: All network requests to Odoo, Shopify, and Polar are made exclusively from the Extension's service worker. The injected panel (content script) never makes cross-origin requests and never receives your credentials.
storage.tokenInMemoryDesc: The Shopify Admin token is held in memory only for the duration of a comparison. It is never written to storage and never exposed to the page or DOM.
storage.localOnlyDesc: Operational data (session info, shop mapping, trial date, license state) is stored locally on your device using Chrome's storage APIs. No order data is transmitted to any third-party server.
storage.transmissionDesc: The Extension communicates directly with YOUR Odoo instance and (via the connector) YOUR Shopify store over HTTPS. Your Odoo session/credentials and order data are never sent to the extension developer or any third party.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.polarIntro: The Extension uses Polar (https://polar.sh/) for subscription management and payment processing. When you purchase a plan through Polar's hosted Checkout, Polar collects:
thirdParty.polar1: Your email address for account management and receipts
thirdParty.polar2: Payment information (processed securely by Stripe, not stored by the Extension)
thirdParty.licenseKeyNote: License activation uses Polar's public license endpoints (validate / activate / deactivate). Your license key is your own secret — you enter it in the Extension's UI, and it is handled only by the service worker. No organization secret is bundled in the Extension.
thirdParty.importantDesc: Payment data is handled entirely by Polar and Stripe. The Extension never receives or stores your credit card information.

dataSharing.title: Third-Party Data Sharing
dataSharing.noShare: We do NOT share your data with any third parties for advertising, analytics, or data brokering purposes.
dataSharing.yourOdooDesc: The Extension reads order, invoicing, and connector data from the Odoo instance you are signed in to
dataSharing.yourShopifyDesc: The Extension reads the matching order from your own Shopify store via the Admin API, using the token obtained from your Odoo connector
dataSharing.polarDesc: Only for subscription management and license validation (email, subscription status, payment processing)
dataSharing.notSell: Sell your data to data brokers
dataSharing.notDeveloper: Send your Odoo or Shopify order data to the extension developer's servers

permissions.title: Permissions Explained
permissions.intro: The Extension requests the following Chrome permissions:
permissions.storage: Required to store the detected Odoo session info, the learned shop-to-instance mapping, the trial install date, and your license activation state locally
permissions.scripting: Required to inject the read-only comparison panel into the order page you are viewing
permissions.alarms: Used to periodically re-validate your license in the background so your subscription state stays current
permissions.hostOdoo: Required to read order and connector data from your Odoo Online or Odoo.sh instance (e.g., yourcompany.odoo.com)
permissions.hostPolar: Required for license validation, subscription management, and the hosted checkout / customer portal
permissions.whyIntro: Every permission maps directly to a feature you can see:
permissions.why1: Host permissions are ONLY used to talk to your Odoo instance and to Polar for licensing
permissions.why2: They are NEVER used to access unrelated websites, and no Shopify host permission is requested — Shopify is reached via the connector configured in your Odoo
permissions.why3: You can revoke any permission at any time in Chrome settings, or remove the Extension entirely
permissions.noteDesc: The Extension only reads the order data needed to build the comparison. It never writes to Odoo or Shopify, and it sends nothing to the developer or any unrelated third party.

rights.title: Your Data Rights
rights.intro: You have the following rights regarding your data:
rights.accessDesc: All order data lives in your own Odoo instance and Shopify store, which you control; the Extension only reads it
rights.deletionDesc: You can clear all locally stored Extension data by removing the Extension from Chrome
rights.portabilityDesc: Your data remains in Odoo and Shopify and is accessible via their own export features
rights.controlDesc: You can revoke the Extension's access at any time by removing it or revoking its permissions in Chrome

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.intro: The Extension retains data as follows:
retention.localDataDesc: Session info, shop mapping, trial date, and license state are stored until you remove the Extension
retention.cachedDataDesc: Transient comparison data is kept only briefly to keep the panel responsive and is not persisted
retention.orderDataDesc: Order details are never stored beyond the transient cache used to render the current comparison
retention.uninstall: When you uninstall the Extension, all locally stored data is automatically removed by Chrome.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.desc: The Extension is a business tool and is not intended for use by children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13.

changes.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
changes.desc1: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. We will notify users of any material changes by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this policy and, if required, through the Extension's update notifications.
changes.desc2: Continued use of the Extension after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated privacy policy.

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.desc: If you have questions or concerns about this privacy policy or the Extension's data practices, please contact:

legal.title: Legal Disclaimer
legal.desc1: Odoo–Shopify Order Check is an independent extension and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Odoo S.A. or Shopify Inc.
legal.desc2: Odoo is a trademark of Odoo S.A.; Shopify is a trademark of Shopify Inc.

v100.fieldByFieldDesc: Compare any order between Odoo and Shopify, field by field, right where you work.
v100.verdictDesc: A clear match or mismatch verdict for the whole order at a glance.
v100.invoicedDesc: The verdict is based on Odoo's net invoiced amount (invoices minus refunds), so partial invoices and refunds don't look like errors.
v100.breakdownDesc: Detailed differences across totals, taxes, quantities, line items and addresses.
v100.contextDesc: Refunds, returns and order edits are shown in context, with detection of cancelled orders.
v100.autodetectDesc: The Odoo instance and database are detected automatically from your active session — nothing to register.
v100.settingsDesc: An icon popup and a panel gear let you activate a license, buy, manage your subscription and view read-only diagnostics.
v100.trialDesc: Every install includes a 14-day free trial with full functionality.
v100.plansDesc: After the trial, choose Starter, Growth or Agency — company-wide plans scoped by Odoo instance and Shopify stores, licensed via Polar.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Set up in under a minute — no code, no API key, no configuration required

step1.title: Install
step1.description: Add the extension from the Chrome Web Store with one click

step2.title: Open an order
step2.description: Open any order in Odoo or Shopify. You're already signed in, so there's nothing to configure.

step3.title: Read the verdict
step3.description: The panel appears with a match/mismatch verdict and a field-by-field breakdown

howItWorks.sessionNote: Uses your existing Odoo session to detect the instance automatically. If it ever expires, the extension just asks you to sign back into Odoo — nothing else to manage.
